channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Jimmy Carter eulogized by Kamala Harris, congressional leaders at US Capitol
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington for three days of state funeral rites starting Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, JONATHAN J. COOPER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man who exploded Tesla Cybertruck outside Trump hotel in Las Vegas used generative AI, police say
item.description: The highly decorated soldier who exploded a Tesla Cybertruck outside the Trump hotel in Las Vegas used generative AI including ChatGPT to help plan the attack, Las Vegas police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Orleans is a city marked by tragedy. But don’t call locals resilient
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — She ran around in silver sparkling shoes, her faux chainmail tunic shimmering in the freezing breeze, maneuvering horses made of paper mâché, a giant green dragon, and sheep constructed from milk cartons.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, SHARON LURYE, CLAIRE GALOFARO and SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader says his son-in-law has been kidnapped in Caracas
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Self-exiled Venezuelan opposition leader Edmundo González, who claims to have defeated President Nicolás Maduro in last year’s presidential election, said his son-in-law was kidnapped Tuesday in Venezuela's capital.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Appeals court rejects Trump's latest attempt to get Friday's hush money sentencing called off
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is still set to be sentenced in his hush money case this week — at least for now — after a New York appeals court judge on Tuesday swiftly rejected his second attempt to get it called off.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: How to stay safe during Southern California’s latest Santa Ana wind event
item.description: Whether you live in these communities or adjacent, officials are urging residents to take precautions to stay safe during the latest wind event.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, NewsNation

item.title: As MLK holiday nears, Bernice King urges people to really focus on his teachings
item.description: As the holiday honoring the slain civil rights leader Martin Luther King Jr. approaches, his daughter is urging people to really focus on his work throughout the next year, rather than just paying him tribute for a day.
item.dc:creator: Kate Brumback, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Palisades Fire in Los Angeles scorches 1,262 acres, destroys homes
item.description: The entire community of Pacific Palisades is being told to "evacuate now" due to a rapidly spreading wildfire in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: These will be the most competitive housing markets in 2025, Zillow report predicts
item.description: The hottest housing markets this year are predicted to be on the East Coast, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New York appeals court denies halting Trump's sentencing
item.description: A New York appellate judge on Tuesday declined to suspend President-elect Trump’s Friday sentencing on his hush money criminal conviction.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump won't commit to not using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday refused to commit to not using the U.S. military to gain control of the Panama Canal, after vowing last month to take over the operation of the key passageway.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rapper Trae tha Truth reunited with daughter after abduction
item.description: On New Year’s Day, Trae received the news he had been praying for: Truth was found safe near the Mexican border in California.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Girl Scouts getting rid of 2 cookie offerings
item.description: The Girl Scouts of the USA have announced plans to discontinue two of their “beloved” cookie offerings after the current cookie season, which kicked off Tuesday. The organization revealed the news in a press release issued that morning.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: New Orleans attack came at a time of political heat, and transition, for the FBI
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Joining law enforcement officials to update the public in the hours after a man killed 14 people and injured dozens of others in New Orleans, Louisiana's junior Republican senator, John Kennedy, implored the FBI to “catch these people" — and then added one more eyebrow-raising requ
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Girl Scouts discontinuing 2 cookie varieties after 2025 season
item.description: The Girl Scouts of the USA have announced plans to discontinue two of their “beloved” cookie offerings after the current cookie season, which kicked off Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tibet earthquake kills at least 126 people and leaves many trapped
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — A strong earthquake shook a high-altitude region of western China and areas of Nepal on Tuesday, damaging hundreds of houses, littering streets with rubble and killing at least 126 people in Tibet. Many others were trapped as dozens of aftershocks shook the remote region.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Donations received to fly migrant girl who lost legs on Mexican train and family
item.description: Inside the Humanitarian Respite Center on Monday a family of a little girl who lost her legs on a train coming to the border were preparing to leave the border after an outpour of donations were sent to help the family.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Biden creating two national monuments in California
item.description: President Biden is creating two new national monuments in California, the White House announced Tuesday. Biden will officially designate the Chuckwalla National Monument and the Sáttítla Highlands National Monument — protecting a combined 848,000 acres of particular significance to tribes.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WGN 9

item.title: Meta replaces fact-checking with X-style community notes
item.description: Facebook and Instagram owner Meta said Tuesday it's scrapping its third-party fact checking program and replacing it with a Community Notes program written by users similar to the model used by Elon Musk's social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Meta replaces fact-checking with community notes
item.description: (AP) — Facebook and Instagram owner Meta said Tuesday it's scrapping its third-party fact checking program and replacing it with a Community Notes program written by users similar to the model used by Elon Musk's social media platform X. Starting in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Father of hospital NICU attack victim says babies had one thing in common
item.description: A Virginia dad whose newborn stayed at the Henrico Doctors' Hospital's NICU in 2023 says more than a half dozen victims of mysterious fractures have one thing in common.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Immigration is a higher priority for Americans than it was a year ago, an AP-NORC poll shows
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More Americans say immigration should be a top focus for the U.S. government in 2025, as the country heads toward a new Republican administration in which President-elect Donald Trump has promised the mass deportations of migrants and an end to birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS, HUMERA LODHI and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Two federal death row inmates refuse Biden's commutation in continued fight to prove their innocence
item.description: Two men who were among the 37 federal inmates on death row whose sentences were commuted by President Biden are rejecting clemency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The polar vortex brings its bitter cold to the Southern US
item.description: The plunging polar vortex is bringing subfreezing temperatures to some of the southernmost points of the U.S. A forming storm system is threatening to dump snow on parts of Texas and Oklahoma, while communities in Louisiana and Florida may see frigid cold.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle And Brian Witte, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Harris calls it a 'good day' for democracy as she oversees peaceful certification of Trump's victory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There was no drama on Monday as Kamala Harris oversaw the ceremonial certification of her defeat to Donald Trump. The vice president kept her remarks perfunctory, and she stood with her hands clasped in front of her while the results from each state were read out loud.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: U-Haul stolen in Albuquerque with Nine Inch Nails memorabilia inside
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (KRQE) – It was not the trip one man driving through Albuquerque expected: his U-Haul full of priceless items from one of his favorite bands was stolen. Ryan Jonik said he's out more than $50,000 from the items stolen, not to mention the sentimental value.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Upchurch, NewsNation

item.title: Uber and Lyft to offer driverless rideshare cars
item.description: Customers in Atlanta and Austin, Texas, will be able to hail one of these driverless rideshare cars via the Uber app.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Flight cancellations pile up as major winter storm hits East Coast
item.description: Flight cancellations and delays are stacking up as a major winter storm makes its way across the East Coast.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Congress certifies Trump's election win despite weather threat
item.description: Congress certified President-elect Donald Trump’s election win on Monday as Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) plowed ahead with the vote despite a snowstorm set to hit Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Luigi Mangione hires Harvey Weinstein’s prison consultant Craig Rothfeld: report
item.description: UnitedHealthcare murder suspect Luigi Mangione has retained Craig Rothfeld, a former white-collar inmate turned prison consultant who also worked for Harvey Weinstein.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Orca that carried her dead calf for weeks in 2018 is doing it again
item.description: An endangered Pacific Northwest orca that made global headlines in 2018 for carrying her dead calf for over two weeks is doing so once again following the death of her new calf, in another sign of grief over lost offspring, researchers said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
